title: ufputi
text: ufputi Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field as if the switch /init were used. Explain this code short ufputi (unsigned char* field,unsigned char* buffer,short length); Copy Parameters field The name of a field in your component. To resolve ambiguity, you may qualify the field name with an entity name, for example, field . entity . Instead of the actual field name, you can also use the internal bind name as returned by ugetbind . buffer The data to be put in the field. This data does not have to be zero-terminated. length The number of bytes that should be copied from buffer to field . Use a negative value to indicate that more data is to follow (using additional calls to ufputc ). Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ufput, ufputi, ufputc, and ufputext Value Meaning -1 An error occurred. 0 All data was copied to the field . 1 There is more data to be copied to the field. Occurs when you have specified a negative Length value. Use Library: yrtl Description ufputi is largely equivalent to ufput . The only difference is that ufputi simulates a ProcScript assignment using the /init switch, meaning that it does not set the functions $fieldmod , $occmod , $formmod , or $formdbmod . Just like ufput , you can use ufputi in conjunction with ufputc to transfer data to a field in steps. For more information, see the "ufput" topic. Related Links ufput Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ufputext Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field, allowing the data to exceed the field length. ufputc Copy additional data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ugetbind Get the internal bind name of a Uniface field.
